frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Optimizing receipt of external reward signals as model of animal behaviour.

frameworks.name: homeostatic / multi-dimensional value
frameworks.one_line: Animals minimise distance between current and desired internal states across value dimensions.

frameworks.name: context-sensitive valuation
frameworks.one_line: Valuation depends on internal state context rather than fixed external reward.

core_question: Where does the value signal that drives animal decision-making actually come from?
key_assumptions: Animals possess current and desired internal states
Value is multi-dimensional rather than scalar
External reward functions are not pre-specified for biological systems
key_findings: RL framework leaves open how the external reward function is designed for biological systems
Valuation is context-sensitive in natural environments
Framework accounts for canonical phenomena in psychology, behavioural ecology, and economics
Framework aligns with recent brain imaging findings on value-guided decision-making
interpretations: Rather than optimizing external reward, animals minimise distance to goal across multiple internal value dimensions
Reframing value as internal-state tracking unifies behavioural and neural observations
